openmeter/billing/charges/worker/advance.purpose: Batch auto-advance worker that iterates all customers with eligible charges and calls charges.Service.AdvanceCharges for each; errors are accumulated and joined so one failing customer does not block others.

patterns.name: Fan-out with error accumulation
patterns.description: Loop over all customers, append errors to a slice, and return errors.Join(errs...) — never return on first failure.

patterns.name: Pagination via CollectAll
patterns.description: Use pagination.CollectAll with pagination.NewPaginator to page through all eligible customers without a manual page loop.

patterns.name: Config struct + constructor validation
patterns.description: All dependencies pass via a Config struct; NewAdvancer validates non-nil fields before constructing the struct.
patterns.example: func NewAdvancer(config Config) (*AutoAdvancer, error) { if config.ChargesService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("charges service is required") }; if config.Logger == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("logger is required") }; return &AutoAdvancer{...}, nil }

patterns.name: Advance via service interface only
patterns.description: All charge advancement is driven through charges.ChargeService.AdvanceCharges — never call adapters or Ent directly.

patterns.name: Stable now captured once per batch
patterns.description: Capture time.Now() once at the start of ListCustomersToAdvance and pass it as AdvanceAfterLTE for the whole batch, preventing per-customer clock races.
patterns.example: now := time.Now() // pass now to ListCustomersToAdvance, not time.Now() inside the paginator closure

patterns.name: Structured logging with slog
patterns.description: Use slog.InfoContext/DebugContext/ErrorContext/WarnContext with named slog.String key-value pairs for all log statements.
patterns.example: a.logger.ErrorContext(ctx, "failed to auto-advance charges", slog.String("namespace", cust.Namespace), slog.String("customer_id", cust.ID), slog.String("error", err.Error()))

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; defines AutoAdvancer, All/ListCustomersToAdvance/AdvanceCharges methods, Config, and NewAdvancer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: defaultPageSize=10_000 caps single-page fetches; `now` is captured once at the start of ListCustomersToAdvance to give a stable cutoff for the whole batch run.

anti_patterns: Returning on first customer error — must accumulate and join all errors.
Calling Ent/adapter code directly instead of going through charges.ChargeService.
Introducing context.Background() — always propagate the caller's ctx.
Hard-coding namespaces instead of accepting them as a parameter.
Capturing time.Now() inside the paginator closure rather than once before it.

decisions.decision: Error accumulation instead of early return.
decisions.rationale: A single customer failure should not prevent advancing charges for all other customers; errors.Join collects all failures for a complete report.

decisions.decision: Time captured once per All() call.
decisions.rationale: A single `now` for the whole batch gives consistent cutoff semantics; per-customer clock calls could admit races where newly eligible charges slip in or out mid-run.

openmeter/billing/charges/worker/asyncad.purpose: Event-driven (async) handler that advances charges for a single customer in response to a charges.AdvanceChargesEvent; intended to be wired as a Watermill message handler for the billing-worker.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: The Config struct carries a Validate() error method checking all required fields; New calls it before constructing the Handler.
patterns.example: func New(c Config) (*Handler, error) { if err := c.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &Handler{chargesService: c.ChargesService, logger: c.Logger}, nil }

patterns.name: Single-responsibility Handle method
patterns.description: Handler exposes exactly one Handle(ctx, *AdvanceChargesEvent) error method; no batch logic, no pagination — those live in the advance package.
patterns.example: func (h *Handler) Handle(ctx context.Context, event *charges.AdvanceChargesEvent) error { _, err := h.chargesService.AdvanceCharges(ctx, charges.AdvanceChargesInput{Customer: customer.CustomerID{Namespace: event.Namespace, ID: event.CustomerID}}); return err }

patterns.name: CustomerID constructed from event fields
patterns.description: Build customer.CustomerID{Namespace: event.Namespace, ID: event.CustomerID} inline from the event — no helper, no global state.

patterns.name: Warn-then-return on service error
patterns.description: Log a WarnContext entry with namespace/customer_id/error keys before returning the error so the upstream Watermill router can apply retry/dead-letter logic.
patterns.example: h.logger.WarnContext(ctx, "failed to advance charges", slog.String("namespace", event.Namespace), slog.String("customer_id", event.CustomerID), slog.String("error", err.Error())); return err

key_file_guides.role: Sole file; defines Handler, Config (with Validate), New constructor, and Handle method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Handler must not swallow errors — always return them so the message bus can apply retry/dead-letter logic. Never add batch or pagination logic here.

anti_patterns: Adding batch/pagination logic here — that belongs in the advance package.
Calling Ent or adapter code directly instead of going through charges.ChargeService.
Returning nil on service error to suppress retries.
Introducing context.Background() instead of propagating the event's ctx.
Accumulating errors across multiple events — this handler processes one event per call.

decisions.rationale: Async (event-driven, single-customer) and sync (batch, all-customers) advancement have different error-handling and retry contracts; separate packages avoid conflating them.
Watermill's router applies retry and dead-letter semantics based on the handler's return value; swallowing errors would silently drop failed advancements.

code_examples.scenario: Handle a single AdvanceChargesEvent from the event bus
